Description.Name: Collateral Truths
Description.Details: Darius enters the briefing room with the standard post-mission damage assessment. The datapad shows sensor logs, structural damage reports, and intercepted local news feeds from Niops. "Boss, after-action review is complete. Target eliminated as ordered. Jorgensson's dead. Mission accomplished." He brings up the collateral damage analysis on the main display. "Combat zone assessment shows seventeen confirmed civilian casualties. Caught in the engagement area during mech combat operations. Standard risk profile for populated sector operations." He pulls up one of the intercepted news feeds. "Local authorities confirm seventeen dead following a sudden engagement between two unknown forces in the settlement of Onisville. Structural damage to multiple buildings, some partially collapsed. Emergency services are still assessing the affected area. No statement from planetary defense forces at this time." Farah reviews the casualty data. "Seventeen fatalities. With combat at that scale, in a populated sector, civilian casualties were statistically inevitable." Yang grunts. "Unexpected 'Mech fights near civilians is always a mess. They often don't have enough time to evacuate once all hell breaks loose. Nothing to be done once it starts." Sumire looks over Farah's shoulder at the data. "Seventeen people who had nothing to do with any of this." Darius closes the report. "It's ugly. No one likes civilian casualties. But that's war in the Periphery. Jorgensson chose to operate in a populated sector. We had orders to eliminate him before he reached evac. We did our job." The HPG relay activates. Incoming transmission. Tribe encryption. High priority flagging. The viewscreen flickers to life. Joanna McEvedy appears, expression focused and urgent. "Commander. We have received critical intelligence that requires immediate action." "A [[TGT_SYSTEM.OwnerDef,{TGT_SYSTEM.OwnerDef.Demonym}]] convoy operating in collaboration with [[DM.Factions[faction_GhostBearWatch],Ghost Bear Watch]] is transporting bio-warfare research materials through the frontier region. Our intelligence indicates these shipments are disguised as medical supplies but contain weaponized pathogen samples intended for deployment against Tribe civilian populations." "The convoy is scheduled to transit through a canyon pass sometime this week. This is our only opportunity to intercept before the materials reach their destination settlements." She transmits tactical data. "Expected opposition includes at least one full lance of mechs escorting the transport vehicles. The canyon terrain provides excellent ambush positions with overlapping fields of fire. Isolated location means minimal risk of third-party interference." "This is high priority, Commander. The convoy must not reach the settlements. Complete destruction of all cargo is essential to prevent deployment of these bio-weapons. No trace of the shipment can remain." The transmission ends. Sumire pulls up the canyon topology. "The terrain gives us excellent tactical advantage. We can position on the high ground with overlapping fields of fire. Classic ambush setup. They won't have room to maneuver in that pass." Darius reviews the mission parameters. "Standard convoy interdiction. We've done dozens of these operations. Textbook ambush. Hit them in the kill zone, destroy the transports, extract before reinforcements arrive." Yang checks the mech readiness status. "At least it's not as near a settlement this time. Mountain pass ambush is cleaner. No buildings, no civilians wandering through the engagement zone." Farah analyzes the intelligence package. "If the cargo really is weaponized pathogens, complete destruction is critical. We'll need to ensure total elimination of all transport vehicles." Darius looks to you. "Boss, this is routine work. Destroying enemy supply convoys carrying dangerous materials is standard military operations. The bio-weapons angle makes it high priority, but tactically it's straightforward. Are we ready to deploy?"

Description.Name: Prepare for deployment
Description.Details: You confirm the mission. Darius logs the contract acceptance. "Understood, Commander. Transit to the intercept coordinates is plotted. We'll be in position at the canyon pass with time to establish our ambush before the convoy arrives." Yang heads to the mech bay. "Convoy ambush in a mountain pass. At least it's not urban combat this time. I'll prep the heavy weapons loadouts for vehicle destruction." Sumire coordinates navigation. "Jump sequence calculated. We'll arrive at the intercept point before the convoy's scheduled transit. Plenty of time to scout positions." Farah runs weapons inventory. "All systems operational. If we need to ensure complete cargo destruction, incendiary weapons would be most effective against bio-warfare containment." Darius reviews the tactical plan one last time. [A new contract is available in the Command Center]
